prompt: A woven textile seen from above, filling the frame. The vertical threads are pale, thin, and perfectly evenly spaced — they form a background grid. The horizontal threads are darker, heavier, and vary in spacing and thickness: some clustered tight, some wide apart, some doubling back. But they are clearly the source of the visible pattern in the cloth. The viewer should immediately see that the regular threads are merely structural while the irregular threads create the design. Tight crop on the weave itself, no loom frame, no border.
